planned_changes.description: Request and ingest limit_approval_risk and signoff rationale from the packet owner; update the input sheet (Packet) and re-run calculations to produce end-to-end normalized_risk values.

Modify the spreadsheet to include explicit canonical formula cells for normalized_risk and sensitivity rows; add cell-level annotations (sheet & cell coordinates) and ensure formulas reference the ingested limit_approval_risk cell.

Add a 'navigation & formula index' section to response_plan.md listing key sheet names, exact cell ranges, and pasted formula snippets (or a small CSV excerpt) to enable verification when XLSX parsing is unavailable.

Document the exact sensitivity method in response_plan.md: which fields are shifted by sensitivity_shift_pct (+/-15%), whether shifts apply to numerators or denominators, rounding rules, and how +/-% result ranges were computed.

Add an assumptions table enumerating every referenced packet field (exact field names) and a budget reconciliation table showing decision_budget_usd vs. estimated cost for the proposed intervention; include simple cost derivation for each prioritized record and contingency options.

Commit the builder script (build_ectd.py) and any small helper files to the artifacts for the revised attempt; ensure the builder outputs match the documented cell references and include a sample CSV of computed rows.

Rerun the generator with the ingested denominator, update response_plan.md derived metrics and prioritized table with final numeric values, and record direct cell citations for the normalized_risk and sensitivity ranges.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Close reproducibility gaps and minor documentation gaps: ingest missing limit_approval_risk, add explicit cell references and formula index, document sensitivity method and assumptions, and provide budget reconciliation and contingency options so reviewers and domain experts can validate the full normalized_risk computation.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Close reproducibility gaps and minor documentation gaps: ingest missing limit_approval_risk, add explicit cell references and formula index, document sensitivity method and assumptions, and provide budget reconciliation and contingency options so reviewers and domain experts can validate the full normalized_risk computation.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Strong, auditable baseline. The author preserved reproducible partial calculations, refused to fabricate a missing denominator (limit_approval_risk), provided a clear smallest-defensible intervention (request missing denominator and signoff rationale), and produced both the response_plan.md and an XLSX workbook generator. Limitations: the spreadsheet could not be programmatically parsed in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable) and the packet omitted limit_approval_risk which blocks end-to-end numeric validation of the full normalized_risk formula. Minor documentation gaps remain (explicit cell references, assumptions table, sensitivity method details, and a brief budget reconciliation).
